fileNodes.name: deploy-to-vps.sh
fileNodes.summary: Bash deployment script that syncs the local dist/ build to a VPS over SSH/rsync, creating a timestamped backup of the remote directory before overwriting. Targets ailinksdb.masellas.info.

fileNodes.name: eslint.config.js
fileNodes.summary: Root ESLint flat config enabling TypeScript, React Hooks, and React Refresh rules for all .ts/.tsx files, with dist/ excluded from linting.

fileNodes.name: api-DyDpKlVl.js
fileNodes.summary: Minified build artifact bundling the shared API layer: config constants, UI string constants (Catalan locale), and async functions for fetching/saving bookmarks and categories, plus a Claude AI categorization proxy call.

fileNodes.name: popup-DbKNwE_q.js
fileNodes.summary: Minified build artifact for the extension popup UI, bundling the full React popup component tree. Imports from the api build artifact.

fileNodes.name: service-worker.js
fileNodes.summary: Minified build artifact of the Chrome extension service worker. Handles CHECK_DUPLICATE, SAVE_BOOKMARK, ADD_CATEGORY, and GET_CATEGORIES messages from the popup, with in-memory category caching (5-minute TTL).

fileNodes.name: service-worker.ts
fileNodes.summary: Chrome extension service worker source. Listens for popup messages (CHECK_DUPLICATE, SAVE_BOOKMARK, ADD_CATEGORY, GET_CATEGORIES) and dispatches to the shared API layer. Maintains an in-memory category cache with 5-minute TTL to reduce API round-trips.

fileNodes.name: content.js
fileNodes.summary: Minified build artifact of the content script. Extracts page metadata (title, description, author, URL) from DOM/meta tags and responds to GET_METADATA messages from the extension popup.

fileNodes.name: content.ts
fileNodes.summary: Chrome extension content script source. Scrapes page metadata (title, description, author, URL) from OG/Twitter/standard meta tags with fallback strategies, and exposes it via a chrome.runtime message listener responding to GET_METADATA.

fileNodes.name: index.tsx
fileNodes.summary: Entry point for the Chrome extension popup. Mounts the Popup React component into the DOM root using ReactDOM.createRoot inside React.StrictMode.

fileNodes.name: popup.tsx
fileNodes.summary: Main React component for the browser extension popup. Manages view-state machine (loading, form, duplicate, success, error, tabs, tabs-saving, tabs-summary), handles single-page bookmark saving with AI category suggestion via Claude proxy, and drives bulk tab saving with sequential processing, per-tab status tracking, and retry support.

fileNodes.name: singleSaveUtils.ts
fileNodes.summary: Exports a single utility function implementing a 3-tier category resolution fallback: Claude-validated categories, user-selected categories, or the default 'Altres'.

fileNodes.name: tabsUtils.ts
fileNodes.summary: Collection of pure utility functions and constants supporting the tabs bulk-save feature: tab filtering by group, selection management (toggle/select-all/deselect-all), bookmark construction from a tab, save status summarization, favicon URL generation, and a Tailwind CSS color map for Chrome tab group colors.

fileNodes.name: postcss.config.js
fileNodes.summary: PostCSS configuration for the extension, enabling Tailwind CSS and Autoprefixer plugins.

fileNodes.name: api.ts
fileNodes.summary: Shared API client for the browser extension. Provides typed functions for fetching bookmarks, categories, saving bookmarks and categories via REST API, duplicate URL detection, and calling the local Claude proxy for AI-based categorization with graceful error fallback.

fileNodes.name: config.ts
fileNodes.summary: Central configuration module for the extension exporting API base URL and auth secret, Catalan error message strings, the Claude proxy URL, and all Catalan UI strings including dynamic tab-count message generators.

fileNodes.name: types.ts
fileNodes.summary: Shared TypeScript type definitions for the extension: Bookmark, ExtractedMetadata, Chrome messaging types (Message, MessageResponse), API response shapes, and tabs-feature types (TabItem, TabSaveStatus, TabGroupColor, TabGroupInfo).

fileNodes.name: tailwind.config.js
fileNodes.summary: Tailwind CSS configuration for the extension, scoped to popup directory HTML/JS/TS/TSX files.

fileNodes.name: single-save.test.ts
fileNodes.summary: Vitest unit tests for resolveSaveCategories covering all 3-tier fallback branches: Claude categories in whitelist, Claude categories filtered out, empty Claude result, empty user selection, and partial whitelist filtering.

fileNodes.name: tabs-filter.test.ts
fileNodes.summary: Vitest unit tests for tab filtering utilities. Tests filterTabsByGroup (all tabs, ungrouped-only, specific groupId) and hasGroups from tabsUtils. Uses a local makeTabs factory for isolated test data.

fileNodes.name: tabs-save.test.ts
fileNodes.summary: Vitest unit tests for tab save helper functions. Covers buildTabBookmark (id prefix, url mapping, default categories, timestamp) and getTabSaveSummary (counting saved/failed statuses from a Map).

fileNodes.name: tabs-selection.test.ts
fileNodes.summary: Vitest unit tests for tab selection utilities. Tests toggleTabSelection (add/remove/immutability), selectAllVisible, deselectAllVisible, and getSelectionCount from tabsUtils.

fileNodes.name: vite.config.ts
fileNodes.summary: Vite build configuration for the browser extension. Defines three entry points (popup HTML, content script TS, service worker TS) with custom output path rules and a manualChunks strategy to inline shared modules into the service worker and content script.

fileNodes.name: vitest.config.ts
fileNodes.summary: Vitest configuration for the extension test suite. Sets jsdom as the test environment and enables global test APIs.

fileNodes.name: deploy.sh
fileNodes.summary: Bash deployment script for the mobile PWA. Builds the project via npm then rsync-transfers the dist/ output to the VPS path /var/www/ailinksdb.masellas.info/mobile/ with --delete to clean stale files.

fileNodes.name: postcss.config.js
fileNodes.summary: PostCSS configuration for the mobile PWA. Enables Tailwind CSS and autoprefixer plugins.

fileNodes.name: sw.js
fileNodes.summary: Minimal PWA service worker. Handles install (skipWaiting), activate (clients.claim), and fetch (passthrough) events to satisfy PWA installability requirements without any caching strategy.

fileNodes.name: App.tsx
fileNodes.summary: Root React component for the mobile PWA share-target flow. Manages a ViewState machine (loading/form/duplicate/success/error), loads shared URL/title from query params, fetches categories from the API, handles category selection and creation, validates and saves bookmarks with duplicate detection.

fileNodes.name: api.ts
fileNodes.summary: Thin re-export barrel for the shared extension API. Forwards getBookmarks, getCategories, saveBookmark, and isDuplicate from the shared extension module so the mobile PWA can consume the same API surface without duplication.

fileNodes.name: config.ts
fileNodes.summary: Mobile PWA configuration constants: ERRORS object with Catalan-language user-facing error strings, and UI_STRINGS object with all static UI label and message strings. Serves as the single source of truth for i18n text in the mobile app.

fileNodes.name: main.tsx
fileNodes.summary: PWA entry point: registers the service worker at /mobile/sw.js if supported, then mounts the React App component into the DOM root using React 18 createRoot with StrictMode.

fileNodes.name: utils.test.ts
fileNodes.summary: Vitest unit tests for mobile utility functions. Covers resolveAuthorFromUrl (GitHub, Twitter/X, and fallback cases) and parseShareParams (url param, text param fallback, and empty input cases).

fileNodes.name: utils.ts
fileNodes.summary: Mobile PWA utility functions: resolveAuthorFromUrl infers a source label (github/twitter/web) from a URL string; parseShareParams extracts url and title from a Web Share Target query string, falling back to the text param for the URL.

fileNodes.name: tailwind.config.js
fileNodes.summary: Tailwind CSS configuration for the mobile PWA workspace. Scans HTML and all TypeScript/TSX/JS sources under src/ for class names; no theme extensions or plugins configured.

fileNodes.name: vite.config.ts
fileNodes.summary: Vite build configuration for the mobile PWA: uses the React plugin, sets the public base path to /mobile/, outputs to dist/, and configures the jsdom test environment for Vitest.

fileNodes.name: postcss.config.js
fileNodes.summary: Root PostCSS configuration enabling Tailwind CSS and Autoprefixer plugins for the main web app workspace.

fileNodes.name: install.sh
fileNodes.summary: macOS LaunchAgent installer script for the local Claude AI proxy server. Detects the Node.js binary path, substitutes path placeholders in a .plist template, writes the plist to ~/Library/LaunchAgents, and loads it via launchctl so the proxy starts on port 3838 at login.

fileNodes.name: server.js
fileNodes.summary: Express.js proxy server that bridges the React app to the Claude AI CLI. Exposes two POST endpoints: /categorize (handles both tweet URLs and regular pages, generating title/description/categories via Claude) and /process-tweet (processes raw tweet objects into structured bookmark data). Uses child_process.spawn to invoke the claude CLI with JSON schema-constrained output. Designed for testability: createApp factory separates construction from port binding, and getChildEnv strips Claude Code session variables to allow nested invocation.

fileNodes.name: mock-claude.sh
fileNodes.summary: Minimal bash mock of the claude CLI binary used in proxy tests. Inspects argument string to decide which JSON response shape to echo: categorization response or tweet processing response. Avoids spawning the real Claude CLI during automated tests.

fileNodes.name: test-install.sh
fileNodes.summary: Bash test script that validates the launchd plist template (com.ailinks.claude-proxy.plist) by substituting __HOME__ and __PROXY_DIR__ placeholders and verifying no placeholders remain and the actual HOME path is present. Exits with SKIP if the plist template does not yet exist.

fileNodes.name: split-tweets.sh
fileNodes.summary: Bash utility that splits a large Twitter bookmark JSON file into batches of 10 tweets, sorted chronologically (oldest first). Uses an embedded Python 3 script for JSON parsing and date handling. Supports both Unix timestamp and Twitter date string formats. Creates a named output directory with zero-padded numbered JSON files.

fileNodes.name: App.tsx
fileNodes.summary: The central React component of the AI Bookmark Manager (1909 lines). Manages the full application lifecycle: importing Twitter JSON archives, processing tweets through the Claude proxy, deduplication/blacklist logic, category management with drag-and-drop reordering, bookmark CRUD (create/edit/delete), highlight toggling, search with URL sync, data persistence via storage service, JSON export/backup, and a multi-step review carousel for non-AI tweets. Also defines the BookmarkCard sub-component for rendering individual bookmarks with neobrutalist styling.

fileNodes.name: ScrollToTop.tsx
fileNodes.summary: React component that renders a fixed-position scroll-to-top button (neobrutalist yellow with ArrowUp icon). Becomes visible when the user scrolls more than 400px down, using a scroll event listener attached/detached via useEffect.

fileNodes.name: UI.tsx
fileNodes.summary: Design system primitives for the neobrutalist UI. Exports: Button (4 variants: primary/secondary/danger/ghost with shadow/active effects), Card (bordered container with optional title), Input, TextArea, Label, Select (with custom SVG chevron), Badge (colored pill), and Modal (backdrop + z-index priority support). All components use Tailwind CSS utility classes.

fileNodes.name: main.tsx
fileNodes.summary: React application entry point. Mounts the App component inside React.StrictMode onto the DOM element with id='root'. Throws an error if the root element is not found.

fileNodes.name: claudeService.test.ts
fileNodes.summary: Vitest test suite for claudeService.ts covering: module export shape verification, successful proxy response mapping to ProcessedTweetResult[], fallback bookmark creation when proxy is unreachable (fetch failure), and AbortError propagation when AbortSignal is pre-aborted. Uses vi.stubGlobal to mock fetch and importMeta.env.

fileNodes.name: claudeService.ts
fileNodes.summary: Handles communication with the local Claude proxy server to process raw tweets. Iterates tweets one at a time (BATCH_SIZE=1), sanitizes text, POSTs to /process-tweet with retry logic (up to 3 attempts), and generates fallback results when the proxy is unavailable.

fileNodes.name: storage.ts
fileNodes.summary: Provides a unified storage abstraction that routes reads/writes to either a VPS REST API (when VITE_STORAGE_SECRET is set) or browser localStorage. Manages bookmarks, categories, and deleted IDs with get/save/clear operations.

fileNodes.name: translations.ts
fileNodes.summary: Centralised i18n strings for the UI, primarily in Catalan. Covers app labels, log messages, modal text, alert confirmations, default category lists, and the AI system prompt template used for Gemini/Claude categorization.

fileNodes.name: types.ts
fileNodes.summary: Defines the core TypeScript interfaces for the application: TweetRaw (raw Twitter export shape), Bookmark (persisted entity), Category (string alias), ProcessedTweetResult (AI output), and LogEntry (import log).

fileNodes.name: vite-env.d.ts
fileNodes.summary: TypeScript ambient declarations for Vite's import.meta.env, exposing VITE_API_KEY, VITE_STORAGE_API_URL, and VITE_STORAGE_SECRET as typed environment variables.

fileNodes.name: vite.config.ts
fileNodes.summary: Vite configuration for the main web app. Enables React plugin, sets dev server to port 3000 on all interfaces, injects GEMINI_API_KEY into process.env, and adds a root path alias '@'.

fileNodes.name: tailwind.config.js
fileNodes.summary: Root Tailwind CSS configuration. Scans all HTML and src files for class usage with no custom theme extensions or plugins.

fileNodes.name: vite.config.ts
fileNodes.summary: Root Vite/Vitest configuration file. Enables the React plugin and sets the test environment to 'node'.
